channel.title: The Sentinel Review — National Security Feed
channel.description: Automated national security news aggregation. Updated every 8 hours.

item.title: 📍 The Dollar Was—and Remains—an Accident | Foreign Policy
item.description: As a new book shows, the dollar isn’t really the United States’ currency, but a 500-year-old relic.

item.title: 🔐 Software patches from Army hackathon going straight to troops in CENTCOM | Defense One
item.description: Project Jailbreak is helping to get contractors open up their systems to each other.

item.title: 🌍 Trump Declares He Is Lifting The Naval Blockade On Iran | The War Zone
item.description: Why the blockade would be lifted without a deal being signed isn't clear and Iranian media is pushing back against his claims. The post Trump Declares He Is Lifting The Naval Blockade On Iran appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🔐 Authoritarian Transformation in Istanbul’s Old City | Foreign Policy
item.description: Two new books seek to capture the changing reality of Turkey and the world.

item.title: 🏛️ US Navy to explore powering shore installations with aircraft carriers | Defense News
item.description: Acting Navy Secretary Hung Cao revealed that Naval Station Norfolk will be “powered from an aircraft carrier" sometime this summer.

item.title: 🏴 Russian Drone Impacts Apartment Building In NATO State Romania, Injuring Civilians | The War Zone
item.description: The incident raises fresh questions about NATO’s ability to counter even very small volumes of Russian drones. The post Russian Drone Impacts Apartment Building In NATO State Romania, Injuring Civilians appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🌍 Data brokers are helping enemies target US troops. The Pentagon must step up, lawmakers say | Defense One
item.description: Commercial location data has been used to "target or surveil U.S. personnel in theater,” CENTCOM says.

item.title: 🏛️ Containerized Variant Of Navy’s Drone-Swatting HELIOS Laser Being Pushed By Congress | The War Zone
item.description: HELIOS is the only laser able to down drones installed on an active Navy warship, and putting it in a container would open up new deployment possibilities. The post Containerized Variant Of Navy’s Drone-Swatting HELIOS Laser Being Pushed By Congress appeared first on The War…

item.title: 📍 SpaceX wins $4.16B Space Force contract to detect airborne moving targets | Breaking Defense
item.description: “While this OTA agreement establishes initial SB-AMTI capability, the Space Force anticipates issuing multiple awards in the coming year ,” the service said today.

item.title: 🏴 What in the World? | Foreign Policy
item.description: Test yourself on the week of May 23: The U.S. strikes Iran, European countries summon Russian diplomats, and the WHO calls for a cease-fire in Congo.

item.title: 🌍 How the US can use its natural gas abundance to ease rising fertilizer prices | Atlantic Council
item.description: The price of fertilizers, many of which are derived from natural gas, have increased due to the conflict in Iran, putting food security at risk. The US has tools to help ease the crisis. The post How the US can use its natural gas abundance to ease rising fertilizer prices…

item.title: 🔐 Army sent jailbroken tech to Middle East as part of ongoing hackathon | Breaking Defense
item.description: The hackathon now allows the Army to take new systems and work them into command and control structures to sync up with radars and sensors, all of which have previously never communicated with each other, according to Army Secretary Dan Driscoll.

item.title: 📍 Inspector O and the Washington Swamp Gas Machine | 38 North
item.description: The bench at the Fontana dell’Acqua Paola overlooking Rome on a pleasant day might have ... The post Inspector O and the Washington Swamp Gas Machine appeared first on 38 North.

item.title: 🌏 US arms sales pause would push Taiwan toward asymmetric-defense tech: analysts | Defense News
item.description: The idea of Trump using arms sales as a negotiating chip with China has sparked concerns that Washington might decline procurement deals with Taiwan.

item.title: 🌍 How far is there to go until the US and Iran end the war? | Defense News
item.description: A tentative deal has been reached to end the war and restart transit in the Strait of Hormuz, and then negotiations on more difficult issues would begin.

item.title: 🏴 Can the EU’s mutual-defense clause replace NATO’s Article 5? | Atlantic Council
item.description: The European Union's mutual-defense clause, less well-known than NATO's commitment under Article 5 that an attack on one is an attack on all, is receiving new attention in light of questions over the US security commitment to Europe. The post Can the EU’s mutual-defense clause…

item.title: 🌍 Syria Wants to Replace the Strait of Hormuz | Foreign Policy
item.description: The country hopes to fund its reconstruction by serving as the Middle East’s new transit and logistics hub.

item.title: 🌏 China is building launch pads near its nuclear missile silos | Defense News
item.description: China’s nuclear missiles can reach any U.S. city. Now, satellite images show Beijing is building a sprawling web of military sites near its nuclear silos.

item.title: 🏴 The race for Madagascar has already started | Atlantic Council
item.description: Often overlooked by Western policymakers, Madagascar holds major critical mineral reserves and sits astride key maritime routes. As Russia deepens ties, the United States can’t afford to cede strategic ground. The post The race for Madagascar has already started appeared first…

item.title: 🏛️ SPACECOM exploring tech for future offensive cislunar ops: Chief Scientist | Breaking Defense
item.description: An analyst called the new commitment to pursue cislunar operations a “massive policy change” for the Pentagon.

item.title: 🌍 Of Course It’s a War | Foreign Policy
item.description: Trump’s legal evasion regarding Iran reveals the rotten state of constitutional war powers.

item.title: 🏴 Romania says Russian drone hit apartment block, NATO vows to defend alliance territory | Defense News
item.description: “I affirmed that NATO stands ready to defend every inch of Allied territory,” NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte said on X.

item.title: 🏴 Ukraine would gain advantage over Russian glide bombs with Gripen-Meteor combo | Defense News
item.description: Moscow has been launching record numbers of glide bombs in 2026, according to the Ukrainian Ministry of Defence, grinding down frontline positions.

item.title: 🌍 The Search for the Missing in Syria: Learning from the Past | Just Security
item.description: The head of Syria's Independent Institution of Missing Persons (IIMP) offers analysis and recommendations for a meaningful justice process. The post The Search for the Missing in Syria: Learning from the Past appeared first on Just Security.

item.title: 🛡️ The Pretext Behind the Trump Administration Labeling Cuba a State Sponsor of Terrorism | Just Security
item.description: Cuba is not a state sponsor of terrorism. Its inclusion on the list reflects changing U.S. policy and the Trump administration's politicization of the "terrorist" designation. The post The Pretext Behind the Trump Administration Labeling Cuba a State Sponsor of Terrorism…

item.title: 🕵️ EXCLUSIVE: OMB could use $1.9B in reconciliation to buy foreign-made ships | Breaking Defense
item.description: “The fact is, no one spends $1.85 billion studying something. That money is there for procurement of assets,” an OMB official told Breaking Defense in an interview.

item.title: 📍 New Zealand budget spares defense, intelligence agencies from cuts | Defense News
item.description: “The rules-based global system is under strain. Countries are boosting their spending on defense," said Finance Minister Nicola Willis.

item.title: 📍 What’s Behind the EU-Mexico Trade Deal? | Foreign Policy
item.description: With USMCA on ice, Mexico City is looking across the Atlantic for new partners.

item.title: 🏛️ Pentagon looks to reinvent the bunker-buster bomb | Defense News
item.description: The goal is to go beyond the traditional brute-force approach of giant bombs and instead manipulate shock waves to destroy targets.

item.title: 🌍 Early Edition: May 29, 2026 | Just Security
item.description: Signup to receive the Early Edition in your inbox here. A curated weekday guide to major news and developments over the last 24 hours. Here’s today’s news: IRAN WAR – CEASEFIRE Vice President JD Vance said yesterday that the United States and Iran are very close to a memorandum…
